data.content:
I now have all the information. Here is the per-component breakdown:

---

## Per-Component Breakdown — Issue #1: "Navigation flicker when transitioning Detail → Detail on tablets"

| Component | What the issue touches / Symptom or proposed fix |
|---|---|
| **UI** | Navigating from one Detail screen to another Detail screen on tablets causes a visible flicker; reproduced on Galaxy Tab S8 in fold-to-tablet mode and has been present since Compose BOM 2023-11. |
| **Navigation** | The flicker occurs specifically during Detail → Detail transitions, suggesting the navigation graph or transition animation does not cleanly handle re-entry into the same destination type on large-screen layouts. |
| **Networking** | The network log shows two `getCharacter` requests fire on the second tap even though the first response has already returned, indicating duplicate or redundant network calls during the transition. |
| **ViewModel (logic)** | The ViewModel launches a new coroutine on each tap instead of cancelling/de-duplicating the previous one; a commenter proposes switching from `launchIn` to `flatMapLatest` so the earlier request is automatically cancelled. |
| **Build / Gradle** | The regression is traced back to Compose BOM 2023-11, implying a dependency-version change (BOM upgrade) may be the root trigger of the flicker and double-request behaviour. |
| **Tests** | No explicit test failures or test-related discussion appear in the issue, but the report originates from a QA build, suggesting the behaviour was caught during manual QA and lacks automated coverage for this transition path. |
